articles.title: Location-Based Entertainment Market Sees Strong Global Growth
articles.summary: A new market report confirms the location-based entertainment sector is expanding rapidly worldwide, signaling strong demand for FEC-style venues. The growth reflects increasing consumer appetite for out-of-home, experience-driven entertainment. FEC operators and investors should take note as the expanding market may attract new competition and capital.

articles.title: TrainerTainment Brings Bowling Center Growth Strategies to Bowl Expo 2026
articles.summary: TrainerTainment, powered by The GrowthPro Group, will exhibit at Bowl Expo 2026 in Nashville from June 28 through July 2, focusing on actionable growth strategies for bowling center operators. The company is showcasing real-world success stories from centers that have improved revenue and guest experience. Bowl Expo remains a key annual event for FEC operators with a bowling component.

articles.title: Industry Leaders Outline Design Principles for Modern Family Attractions
articles.summary: At the Festival of Innovation 2026, attraction industry leaders outlined key principles for successful family experiences, including active guest participation, immersive storytelling, and technology that serves narrative rather than acting as a standalone gimmick. Revenue drivers such as themed food, retail, and premium experiences were highlighted as critical to long-term financial health. FEC operators can apply these insights directly to attraction design, programming, and ancillary revenue strategies.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Opens at Potomac Mills Mall in Northern Virginia
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park has opened a new location at Potomac Mills, one of the largest malls in the mid-Atlantic region, bringing its indoor slide-based attraction concept to a high-traffic retail environment. The opening is part of Slick City's broader expansion strategy targeting mall-anchored entertainment. This is a notable example of the indoor active entertainment trend filling vacancy in retail centers.

articles.title: Largest Indoor Slide Attraction Positions Itself as Major Family Tourism Draw
articles.summary: A new large-scale indoor slide attraction in the United States is being positioned as a significant challenger to traditional family tourism destinations, drawing comparisons to theme park-level experiences. The development underscores the growing consumer appetite for mega-scale indoor entertainment options. FEC operators should monitor this trend as large-format indoor attractions continue to raise the bar for the sector.

articles.title: Plymouth, UK to Get Large New Go-Kart and Laser Tag Entertainment Hub
articles.summary: A new multi-attraction entertainment hub featuring go-karts and laser tag is planned for Plymouth, England, adding to the growing pipeline of FEC-style venues opening across the UK. The development reflects continued investment in indoor active entertainment in secondary markets. Operators and suppliers in the UK market should watch this project as a sign of regional demand.

articles.title: New Indoor Adventure Park Described as Unlike Any Other Opens in Atlanta
articles.summary: A new adventure park billing itself as unlike anything else in the country has opened in the Atlanta area, adding to the competitive FEC landscape in one of the Southeast's largest markets. The venue targets both family and adult audiences with a range of active attractions. Atlanta-area FEC operators should take note of the new competitive entrant.

articles.title: Multi-Million-Pound Entertainment Venue Opens at Brent Cross Shopping Centre
articles.summary: A new large-scale entertainment venue has opened at Brent Cross in London, continuing the trend of major retail centers integrating experiential entertainment to drive foot traffic. The investment signals confidence in the UK indoor entertainment market. FEC operators and developers should watch mall-anchored entertainment deals in the UK as a growing opportunity.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Partners with Toy Foundation for International Day of Play, Offers Free Admission
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese celebrated International Day of Play by offering free Adventure Zone admission and launching a June fundraiser in partnership with The Toy Foundation. The promotion highlights the brand's ongoing strategy of using cause marketing and family-focused events to drive store traffic. FEC operators can draw inspiration from Chuck E. Cheese's community engagement and promotional tactics.

articles.title: Six Flags Great Adventure Launches 'Boardwalk Nights' Summer Evening Entertainment Series
articles.summary: Six Flags Great Adventure has debuted Boardwalk Nights, a new summer evening event at its Shoreline Pier featuring live entertainment, running select nights through August 2. The initiative is designed to extend guest dwell time and boost per-cap spending during evening hours. FEC operators can take note of the strategy of transforming daytime venues into compelling nighttime destinations.

articles.title: Lululand Adventure Park to Debut in Pennsylvania Next Year
articles.summary: Lululand Adventure Park has announced plans to open its first Pennsylvania location, expanding the brand's footprint in the competitive Mid-Atlantic indoor entertainment market. The chain's growth trajectory is worth watching for FEC operators and commercial real estate developers looking at experiential anchors. Details on the specific market and format are expected as the opening date approaches.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Venue Opening Soon in Newburgh, New York
articles.summary: A new entertainment destination described as a hot spot for guests of all ages is set to open in Newburgh, New York, adding indoor entertainment options to the Hudson Valley market. The opening reflects continued investment in family-focused venues in suburban and secondary markets. Local FEC operators should be aware of the new competitive entrant.

articles.title: High Tide VR Near Bethany Beach Offers Immersive Virtual Reality Entertainment
articles.summary: High Tide VR, located near Bethany Beach, Delaware, is attracting attention as a destination offering virtual reality experiences distinct from traditional beach-area entertainment options. The venue illustrates how VR-focused FECs can carve out a niche in tourist-heavy markets. Operators considering VR as a core attraction type can look to this model for insights on positioning and marketing.

articles.title: New Entertainment Facility Set to Open in Springfield, Illinois in July
articles.summary: A new indoor entertainment facility is scheduled to open in Springfield, Illinois this July, bringing additional family-focused options to the central Illinois market. The opening is part of a broader national trend of FEC investment in mid-size markets. Local operators should prepare for increased competition as consumer expectations for entertainment variety continue to rise.

articles.title: Fort Worth's Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center Closes After Decades
articles.summary: Tornado Terry's Family Amusement Center in Fort Worth, Texas has announced its closure, ending a long run as a local entertainment staple. The closure highlights ongoing challenges faced by independently owned FECs in competing with larger branded entertainment venues. The story serves as a cautionary example for operators navigating cost pressures and evolving consumer preferences.

articles.title: Family-Owned Fort Worth FEC to Shut Down After Three Decades
articles.summary: A family-owned entertainment business in Fort Worth is closing after approximately 30 years of operation, citing challenges that have become common for independent FEC operators. The closure follows a broader pattern of smaller venues struggling against well-capitalized competitors and shifting consumer expectations. Industry observers should note the recurring theme of succession planning and reinvestment challenges for legacy FEC businesses.

articles.title: Floyd Bowling & Amusement Center Demonstrates How Bowling Remains a Serious Business
articles.summary: A profile of Floyd Bowling & Amusement Center in Rome, Georgia highlights how bowling-anchored FECs continue to thrive when operators treat the business with professionalism and community focus. The center's approach offers a useful case study for multi-attraction venues looking to maintain bowling as a core revenue driver. Operators can draw lessons on programming, customer retention, and operational discipline.

articles.title: Canada's Wonderland Expands Fireworks Lineup and Launches Flexible Membership Program for Summer 2026
articles.summary: Canada's Wonderland is enhancing its summer 2026 offering with five fireworks nights in July and a new subscription-style membership program designed to encourage repeat visitation. The flexible membership model reflects a growing trend among large parks to move toward recurring revenue structures. FEC operators of all sizes can take note of the membership and event-driven traffic strategies being deployed by major park operators.

articles.title: Kings Dominion Puts Live Entertainment at Center of Summer 2026 Guest Experience
articles.summary: Kings Dominion is elevating its Summer 2026 programming with expanded live shows including Cirque Imagine and a new interactive water parade, positioning live entertainment as a primary draw alongside rides. The strategy underscores the industry's shift toward experiential programming to differentiate venues and increase dwell time. FEC operators can consider how live entertainment programming can elevate the overall guest experience and drive repeat visits.

articles.title: Alabama Adventure Brings Back Iconic Rampage Wooden Coaster and Teases Major Announcement
articles.summary: Alabama Adventure & Splash Adventure is reopening its beloved Rampage wooden roller coaster after an extended closure, responding to years of guest requests. The park is also teasing a significant upcoming announcement that could signal further investment. The revival illustrates the value of legacy attractions as loyalty drivers and the marketing power of responding visibly to guest demand.

articles.title: Coaster Con 48 Draws 630+ Roller Coaster Enthusiasts to Agawam, Massachusetts
articles.summary: More than 630 members of American Coaster Enthusiasts gathered in Agawam, Massachusetts for Coaster Con 48, the organization's annual celebration of roller coasters and amusement park culture. The event drives significant visitation and fan engagement for host parks and the broader amusement industry. Enthusiast conventions like Coaster Con represent a valuable community-building and PR opportunity for parks and suppliers.

articles.title: Chamber Members Tour New Bear River Entertainment Zone in Northern California
articles.summary: Business leaders from Fortuna and Eureka, California toured a new entertainment zone at Bear River, exploring how the development could serve as an economic driver for the region. The project represents a growing trend of tribal and community-backed entertainment developments in rural and semi-rural markets. FEC developers and operators should note the increasing role of non-traditional entities in building out regional entertainment infrastructure.

articles.title: Oregon Robot Dealers Automate Business Operations While Claiming Jobs Are Safe
articles.summary: New robotics dealers operating in Oregon are helping businesses automate front-of-house and back-of-house operations, with vendors asserting that automation complements rather than replaces human workers. For FEC operators facing ongoing labor cost pressures, robotics and automation solutions represent a growing toolkit for managing staffing challenges. The article provides useful context on how automation is being positioned and sold to experience-based businesses.

articles.title: How Sound Design Shapes Guest Perception and Immersion in Attraction Environments
articles.summary: A thoughtful analysis of how strategic use of sound, even without visible characters or elaborate technology, can profoundly shape guest imagination and emotional connection in attraction settings. Drawing on examples from Disneyland, the piece argues that sound is an underutilized storytelling tool with outsized impact on guest experience. FEC designers and operators can apply these principles to haunted attractions, themed zones, escape rooms, and immersive entertainment environments.

articles.title: PortAventura World Details Sustainability Progress in 2025 Annual Report
articles.summary: PortAventura World has released its 2025 Sustainability Report, outlining advances in energy transition and decarbonisation even as the resort expanded with new business units. The report demonstrates how large entertainment venues are integrating sustainability into core operations without sacrificing growth. FEC operators looking to build credible ESG credentials can use PortAventura's approach as a benchmark.

articles.title: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery Relocates from Holts Summit to Fulton, Missouri
articles.summary: Frostbite's Arcade & Creamery, a small-format arcade and dessert concept, has moved its location from Holts Summit to the nearby community of Fulton, Missouri. The relocation highlights both the flexibility and the site-sensitivity challenges faced by independent FEC operators. Small venue operators can take note of the factors driving the move and how community positioning influences FEC viability.
